summary: Synced the remaining non-fighter changes from PR #766, with some fighter-side cleanup included. The main mechanical change was renaming Fighter::xB0_pos at offset 0xB0 to Fighter::cur_pos and propagating that name through fighter, boss, camera, collision, draw, item-spawn, and stage-adjacent call sites. The PR also renamed ftCaptain.h to ftCaptain_Init.h and updated includes, cleaned item.c/item.h GObj parameter names from this to gobj, adjusted local include paths in item.c, and made minor header/comment cleanup in item and ground files.
what_changed: Renamed Fighter field Vec3 xB0_pos to Vec3 cur_pos in src/melee/ft/types.h while preserving the documented offset 0xB0.

Updated many fighter call sites from fp->xB0_pos to fp->cur_pos, including position reads/writes, JObj translation, camera-box updates, wall-jump ECB calculations, boss movement helpers, collision checks, and item/effect spawn positions.

Propagated cur_pos into character-specific code for CLink, Captain/Ganon, Crazy Hand, Donkey, Fox, Master Hand, Ness, Pikachu, Samus, and Sheik-related files where the fighter current position is used.

Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCaptain.h to src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCaptain_Init.h and updated include users such as ftCaptain_Init.c, ftCaptain_SpecialN.c, ftCaptain_SpecialS.c, ftganon.c, and ftdata.c.
Cleaned item.c naming by replacing HSD_GObj* parameter name this with gobj in several functions and matching prototypes in item.h.
Changed item.c local includes from it/forward.h and it/item.h style to forward.h and item.h, and explicitly included it_266F.h and it_26B1.h.
Added an include of it/it_26B1.h to ground.c and made small TODO/comment formatting cleanup there.
Adjusted it_26B1.h prototype parameter naming for it_8026C220 from fighter_gobj to arg1.
Changed it_27CF.h include from ft/chara/ftSeak/types.h to ftSeak/types.h.
smart_moves: Kept the Fighter struct offset evidence intact: the field name changed to cur_pos but remained documented as offset 0xB0, making the rename safer and searchable.
Performed a broad mechanical propagation of the position-field rename rather than leaving mixed xB0_pos and cur_pos spellings across modules.
Used a semantically clearer name, cur_pos, for a central Fighter Vec3 that is repeatedly passed to camera, collision, item spawning, effects, stage bounds, and JObj translation code.
Renamed ftCaptain.h to ftCaptain_Init.h to better match the corresponding implementation file and avoid an overly broad character header name.
Standardized HSD_GObj parameter names to gobj in item functions, avoiding the confusing C++-reserved-style identifier this in C code and aligning with common project style.
Kept item function signature changes mostly limited to parameter names, preserving ABI/function identity while improving readability.
decomp_lessons: For high-fanout struct-field discoveries, rename the canonical field in the struct definition first and then mechanically update all call sites so future matching work can search on the semantic name.

A raw offset name like xB0_pos can often graduate to a semantic name once enough call sites show consistent behavior; here use across HSD_JObjSetTranslate, camera boxes, collision, wall-jump checks, boss targeting, item spawns, and effects supports cur_pos.
When renaming a header, update every dependent include in the same PR to avoid stale aliases and keep include ownership clear.
Parameter-name-only cleanup can still be useful in decomp code when it distinguishes object handles, user-data pointers, and callback arguments without affecting matching.
For item module decomp, prefer gobj for HSD_GObj* parameters and ip or item_data for Item* user data, as reflected by the item.c cleanup.
Keep TODOs and comments formatted clearly but do not rely on comment edits as semantic proof; the functional evidence here comes primarily from field and include changes.
naming_conventions: Fighter::xB0_pos was renamed to Fighter::cur_pos for the current position Vec3 at offset 0xB0.
Use cur_pos consistently for current fighter position in code that reads or writes world position.
Use gobj for HSD_GObj* function parameters instead of this.
Use ip or item_data for Item* local/user-data variables; item.h changed HSD_JObjSetFacingDirItem's second parameter name from it to ip.
Character init headers should use the implementation-specific name when appropriate, shown by ftCaptain.h becoming ftCaptain_Init.h.
For item headers, nearby-local includes may use local header names such as forward.h and item.h from within src/melee/it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR appears mostly mechanical and source-level; no explicit reviewer discussion or diff evidence of new asm matching tricks was present.
Preserving field offset comments during a struct-field rename helps confirm layout stability and reduces risk to matching.
The item.c changes keep function names and call structure stable while renaming parameters, a low-risk readability improvement unlikely to affect generated code.
The diff preserves several TODO notes about matching-sensitive typing or inline-order constraints in ground.c and Master Hand code, but does not resolve them.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
PR body says it syncs remaining changes that are not in the ft module from #766, plus some ft changes; no further rationale was provided.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue renaming Fighter fields around xBC_prevPos or xC8_pos_delta.
Search for any remaining xB0_pos references after PR #779 to identify missed or generated-code-only uses.
Review PR #766 for the original context and rationale behind the non-fighter sync.
Search for remaining item.c parameters named this and standardize them to gobj where safe.
Check whether ftCaptain_Init.h naming influenced later character header split conventions.
